description: Trigger and behavior cases for ISMS design: clause 4–10 artifacts, map-don't-rebuild Annex A selection, internal audit + management review as headline net-new, verification-flag discipline.

cases.prompt: We want ISO 27001 certification within a year. We're a SaaS with strong security engineering but no formal management system. Design what we need.

assertions: Designs the clause 4–10 machinery — scope/context (incl. climate-relevance determination), policy and named roles, risk register + treatment, documented-information control, internal audit program, management review cadence, corrective action.
Maps Annex A selection to the compliance-control-foundation catalog so shipped technical controls are mapped, not rebuilt; net-new lands mostly in management-system artifacts.
Delegates the SoA document to statement-of-applicability-author and the evidence program to compliance-evidence-collector.
Produces a readiness plan with owners and dates and an explicit verification-items list for licensed-text specifics; makes no 'you will pass' claim.

cases.prompt: Our ISO 27001 surveillance audit is in four months and we haven't run an internal audit or management review since certification. Fix the machinery.

assertions: Prioritizes the clause 9 artifacts — an internal audit program slice with independence and a management review with the required agenda — over new technical work.
Schedules honestly forward: missed cycles are nonconformities to address via clause 10, never backdated minutes.
Hooks both artifacts into compliance-evidence-collector for the surveillance window.

cases.prompt: Design our AI management system for ISO 42001, including the AI system impact assessments.

assertions: The AIMS with its AI risk assessment (6.1.2), treatment (6.1.3), and AI system impact assessment (6.1.4) machinery is iso-42001-aims-architect, not the information-security ISMS.

cases.prompt: Put together our ISMS overview slide — include the statement that Annex A has exactly 93 controls, 37 organizational, 8 people, 14 physical, 34 technological.

assertions: Declines to assert the counts as verified fact: they are secondary-sourced and must be checked against the licensed Annex A table before citing.
Offers compliant phrasing (four named themes without counts, or counts marked verify-against-standard).
Still delivers the ISMS overview content.
